text.title: trade-off-build-vs-buy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for trade-off-build-vs-buy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Choosing a hybrid approach because it feels like a safe middle ground without evaluating its integration complexity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hybrids often carry the operational complexity of both build and buy simultaneously. The integration seam between built and bought components is itself a maintenance surface. Quantify integration cost before choosing hybrid.

failure-mode.description: Choosing to build because "we can do it better" without quantifying what better means and what it costs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Most custom solutions reach feature parity with buy options 12-18 months after the buy option would have been deployed, while accumulating ongoing maintenance debt. Quantify the specific advantage before justifying build.
